intro: This archive contains 38 reports on social-engineering threats from April 8-20, 2026. APT37 (ScarCruft, InkySquid) was the most frequently cited actor, alongside ClickFix and Pushpaganda. The majority of incidents were rated high severity, targeting the cryptocurrency and financial-services sectors, as well as hospitality, individuals, and logistics. These campaigns had a global footprint, with notable focus on the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, and Australia.
